name: cyvcf2 (Fast VCF/BCF Parsing)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Brent Pedersen / University of Utah

auth.notes: Local library; wraps htslib C library

performance.notes: Cython/htslib; 10-100x faster than PyVCF; handles BCF binary format

agent_readiness.notes: Fast VCF/BCF variant file parsing via htslib. from cyvcf2 import VCF. vcf = VCF('variants.vcf.gz'). for variant in vcf: chrom = variant.CHROM; pos = variant.POS; ref = variant.REF; alts = variant.ALT. variant.gt_types → np.array (0=HOM_REF, 1=HET, 2=HOM_ALT, 3=UNKNOWN). variant.gt_depths → per-sample depths. variant.INFO.get('AF') → allele frequency. variant.FORMAT.get('GT'). vcf.samples → list of sample names. Region query: vcf('chr1:1000-2000'). Writer: from cyvcf2 import Writer. w = Writer('out.vcf', vcf). w.write_record(variant). VCF header: vcf.add_info_to_header({'ID': 'MYINFO', 'Type': 'Float', 'Number': '1', 'Description': '...'}).

gotchas: No type annotations — poor IDE support
Variant objects are not stored between iterations — must extract values in loop
Requires htslib compiled — use conda or prebuilt wheels
best_when: Fast iteration over large VCF/BCF files (whole genome variant calling results)
Per-sample genotype matrix extraction from multi-sample VCF
When PyVCF is too slow for large datasets
avoid_when: Complex VCF manipulation (use bcftools or GATK),
When type-safe VCF API is needed
Small VCF files where speed doesn't matter (PyVCF is simpler)
summary: cyvcf2 parses VCF/BCF variant files via htslib (10-100x faster than PyVCF). VCF('file.vcf.gz'): variant.CHROM, .POS, .REF, .ALT, .gt_types (array), .gt_depths, .INFO, .FORMAT. Region query: vcf('chr1:1000-2000'). Writer. MIT by Brent Pedersen. Best for fast whole-genome VCF iteration and genotype matrix extraction. gt_types encoding non-intuitive. Tabix index required for region queries. Objects not persistent across iterations.
